content: Drug-drug interactions (DDIs) occur when one drug alters the pharmacokinetics or pharmacodynamics of another. Pharmacokinetic interactions affect ADME: absorption (antacids reduce fluoroquinolone absorption), distribution (protein binding displacement), metabolism (CYP inhibition/induction), excretion (P-glycoprotein competition). Pharmacodynamic interactions involve additive/synergistic effects (benzodiazepines + opioids = respiratory depression) or antagonism (beta-blockers reverse beta-agonists). Clinically significant DDIs cause therapeutic failure or toxicity. Risk increases with polypharmacy, narrow therapeutic index drugs, and comorbidities. Major databases catalog known interactions; clinical judgment determines relevance.
